conclusion: Identity statement 'Hesperus_is_Phosphorus' is necessarily true.
label: True

premises: For any software bug B, if B is possibly present, the code must be reviewed. Bug Overflow_1 is possibly present. Bug Memory_Leak is necessarily absent. If a bug is necessarily absent, it is not possibly present.
conclusion: The code must be reviewed because of Bug Memory_Leak.
label: False

premises: For any combination C, if C is necessarily locked, it is impossible to open C without the master key. Combination Safe_A is necessarily locked. Thief tries to open Safe_A without the master key.
conclusion: Thief successfully opens Safe_A.
label: False

premises: For any contract C, if C is necessarily binding, no court can possibly invalidate C. Contract Omega is necessarily binding. Court Zenith attempts to invalidate Contract Omega.
conclusion: Court Zenith can possibly invalidate Contract Omega.
label: False

premises: For any entities X and Y, if X is a part of Y, and Y is a part of X, then X is identical to Y. Region A is a part of Region B. Region B is a part of Region A.
conclusion: Region A is identical to Region B.
label: True

premises: For any items X and Y, if X is a proper part of Y, the physical mass of X is strictly less than the physical mass of Y. The wheel is a proper part of the bicycle.
conclusion: The physical mass of the wheel is strictly less than the physical mass of the bicycle.
label: True

premises: For any biological organism O, if organ X is a vital part of O, the removal of X destroys O. The heart is a vital part of Organism Leo. The heart is removed from Organism Leo.
conclusion: Organism Leo is destroyed.
label: True

premises: For any group G, if individual I is a member of G, I is a part of G. If I is a part of G, the actions of I do not necessarily constitute the actions of G. Member Alice is a member of Group Committee. Member Alice signs the document.
conclusion: Group Committee signs the document.
label: Uncertain

premises: For any regions R1 and R2, if R1 is a tangential proper part of R2, R1 is a part of R2 and R1 shares a boundary with the exterior of R2. Room 101 is a tangential proper part of Building Alpha.
conclusion: Room 101 shares a boundary with the exterior of Building Alpha.
label: True

premises: For any two fusions F1 and F2, if they share all their atomic parts, they are identical. Fusion Golem and Fusion Statue share all their atomic parts.
conclusion: Fusion Golem is identical to Fusion Statue.
label: True

premises: For any recipe R, if ingredient I is a part of R, altering I changes the final state of R. Sugar is a part of Recipe Cake. Sugar is altered to Salt.
conclusion: The final state of Recipe Cake is changed.
label: True

premises: For any light beam B, if photon P is a part of B, P travels in the exact same vector as B. Photon Gamma is a part of Light beam Laser_1.
conclusion: Photon Gamma travels in the exact same vector as Light beam Laser_1.
label: True

premises: For any network N, if node X is a critical part of N, the isolation of X fragments N into disjoint sub-networks. Node Core_Router is a critical part of Network Local_Net. Node Core_Router is isolated.
conclusion: Network Local_Net fragments into disjoint sub-networks.
label: True

premises: For any army A, if battalion B is a part of A, the surrender of A legally binds B to surrender. Army Red surrenders. Battalion 5th is a part of Army Red.
conclusion: Battalion 5th is legally bound to surrender.
label: True
